methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vuecason is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Dival Banerjee that has raised $3M+. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, though tight peer comparables at the seed stage remain limited.

Given data constraints, the stage-progression method is the primary applicable framework. Using the $3M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the next round yields an implied valuation of $7.5M. A peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied: while 11 same-sector companies exist, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints in the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Deploying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median point of $8M. The single-method foundation reflects real scarcity of comparable seed-stage robotics firms with disclosed valuations. Key uncertainties include the company's burn rate relative to the $3M raised, progress on product development, and customer traction—data not available in this set. Any refinement would require either disclosed Series A pricing from peer rounds or additional operational metrics from Vuecason itself.
